2287 (encode_types<Context, T...>() << packed_arg_bits);
2288}
2289
2290template <typename Context, typename... T, size_t NUM_ARGS = sizeof...(T)>
2291constexpr auto make_descriptor() -> ullong {
2292 return NUM_ARGS <= max_packed_args ? encode_types<Context, T...>()
2293 : is_unpacked_bit | NUM_ARGS;
2294}
2295
2296template <typename Context, int NUM_ARGS>
2297using arg_t = conditional_t<NUM_ARGS <= max_packed_args, value<Context>,
2298 basic_format_arg<Context>>;
2299
2300template <typename Context, int NUM_ARGS, int NUM_NAMED_ARGS, ullong DESC>
2301struct named_arg_store {
2302 // args_[0].named_args points to named_args to avoid bloating format_args.
2303 arg_t<Context, NUM_ARGS> args[NUM_ARGS + 1u];
2304 named_arg_info<typename Context::char_type> named_args[NUM_NAMED_ARGS + 0u];
2305
2306 template <typename... T>
2307 FMT_CONSTEXPR FMT_ALWAYS_INLINE named_arg_store(T&... values)
2308 : args{{named_args, NUM_NAMED_ARGS}, values...} {
2309 int arg_index = 0, named_arg_index = 0;
2310 FMT_APPLY_VARIADIC(
2311 init_named_arg(named_args, arg_index, named_arg_index, values));
2312 }
2313
2314 named_arg_store(named_arg_store&& rhs) {
2315 args[0] = {named_args, NUM_NAMED_ARGS};
2316 for (size_t i = 1; i < sizeof(args) / sizeof(*args); ++i)
2317 args[i] = rhs.args[i];
2318 for (size_t i = 0; i < NUM_NAMED_ARGS; ++i)
2319 named_args[i] = rhs.named_args[i];
2320 }
2321
2322 named_arg_store(const named_arg_store& rhs) = delete;
2323 auto operator=(const named_arg_store& rhs) -> named_arg_store& = delete;
2324 auto operator=(named_arg_store&& rhs) -> named_arg_store& = delete;
2325 operator const arg_t<Context, NUM_ARGS>*() const { return args + 1; }
2326};
2327
2328// An array of references to arguments. It can be implicitly converted to
2329// `basic_format_args` for passing into type-erased formatting functions
2330// such as `vformat`. It is a plain struct to reduce binary size in debug mode.
2331template <typename Context, int NUM_ARGS, int NUM_NAMED_ARGS, ullong DESC>
2332struct format_arg_store {
2333 // +1 to workaround a bug in gcc 7.5 that causes duplicated-branches warning.
2334 using type =
2335 conditional_t<NUM_NAMED_ARGS == 0,
2336 arg_t<Context, NUM_ARGS>[max_of<size_t>(1, NUM_ARGS)],
2337 named_arg_store<Context, NUM_ARGS, NUM_NAMED_ARGS, DESC>>;
2338 type args;
2339};
2340
2341// TYPE can be different from type_constant<T>, e.g. for __float128.
2342template <typename T, typename Char, type TYPE> struct native_formatter {
2343 private:
2344 dynamic_format_specs<Char> specs_;
2345
2346 public:
2347 FMT_CONSTEXPR auto parse(parse_context<Char>& ctx) -> const Char* {
2348 if (ctx.begin() == ctx.end() || *ctx.begin() == '}') return ctx.begin();
2349 auto end = parse_format_specs(ctx.begin(), ctx.end(), specs_, ctx, TYPE);
2350 if FMT_CONSTEXPR20 (TYPE == type::char_type) check_char_specs(specs_);
2351 return end;
2352 }
2353
2354 template <type U = TYPE,
2355 FMT_ENABLE_IF(U == type::string_type || U == type::cstring_type ||
2356 U == type::char_type)>
2357 FMT_CONSTEXPR void set_debug_format(bool set = true) {
2358 specs_.set_type(set ? presentation_type::debug : presentation_type::none);
2359 }
2360
2361 template <typename FormatContext>
2362 FMT_CONSTEXPR auto format(const T& val, FormatContext& ctx) const
2363 -> decltype(ctx.out());
2364};
2365
2366template <bool B> constexpr bool enforce_compile_checks() {
2367#ifdef FMT_ENFORCE_COMPILE_STRING
2368 static_assert(
2369 FMT_USE_CONSTEVAL && B,
2370 "FMT_ENFORCE_COMPILE_STRING requires format strings to use FMT_STRING");
2371#endif
2372 return true;
2373}
2374
2375template <typename T = int> constexpr auto is_locking() -> bool {
2376 return locking<remove_cvref_t<T>>::value;
2377}
2378template <typename T1, typename T2, typename... Tail>
2379constexpr auto is_locking() -> bool {
2380 return locking<remove_cvref_t<T1>>::value || is_locking<T2, Tail...>();
2381}
2382
2383FMT_API void vformat_to(buffer<char>& buf, string_view fmt, format_args args,
2384 locale_ref loc = {});
2385
2386#if FMT_WIN32
2387FMT_API void vprint_mojibake(FILE*, string_view, format_args, bool);
2388#else // format_args is passed by reference since it is defined later.
2389inline void vprint_mojibake(FILE*, string_view, const format_args&, bool) {}
2390#endif
2391} // namespace detail
2392
2393// The main public API.
2394
2395template <typename T, typename Char = char>
2396using named_arg = detail::named_arg<T, Char>;
2397
2398template <typename Char>
2399FMT_CONSTEXPR void parse_context<Char>::do_check_arg_id(int arg_id) {
2400 // Argument id is only checked at compile time during parsing because
2401 // formatting has its own validation.
2402 if (detail::is_constant_evaluated() && use_constexpr_cast) {
2403 auto ctx = static_cast<detail::compile_parse_context<Char>*>(this);
2404 if (arg_id >= ctx->num_args()) report_error("argument not found");
2405 }
2406}
2407
2408template <typename Char>
2409FMT_CONSTEXPR void parse_context<Char>::check_dynamic_spec(int arg_id) {
2410 using detail::compile_parse_context;
2411 if (detail::is_constant_evaluated() && use_constexpr_cast)
2412 static_cast<compile_parse_context<Char>*>(this)->check_dynamic_spec(arg_id);
2413}
2414
2415FMT_BEGIN_EXPORT
2416
2417// An output iterator that appends to a buffer. It is used instead of
2418// back_insert_iterator to reduce symbol sizes and avoid <iterator> dependency.
2419template <typename T> class basic_appender {
2420 protected:
2421 detail::buffer<T>* container;
2422
2423 public:
2424 using container_type = detail::buffer<T>;
2425
2426 constexpr basic_appender(detail::buffer<T>& buf) : container(&buf) {}
2427
2428 FMT_CONSTEXPR auto operator=(T c) -> basic_appender& {
2429 container->push_back(c);
2430 return *this;
2431 }
2432 FMT_CONSTEXPR auto operator*() -> basic_appender& { return *this; }
2433 FMT_CONSTEXPR auto operator++() -> basic_appender& { return *this; }
2434 FMT_CONSTEXPR auto operator++(int) -> basic_appender { return *this; }
2435};
2436
2437// A formatting argument. Context is a template parameter for the compiled API
2438// where output can be unbuffered.
2439template <typename Context> class basic_format_arg {
2440 private:
2441 detail::value<Context> value_;
2442 detail::type type_;
2443
2444 friend class basic_format_args<Context>;
2445
2446 using char_type = typename Context::char_type;
2447
2448 public:
2449 class handle {
2450 private:
2451 detail::custom_value<Context> custom_;
2452
2453 public:
2454 explicit handle(detail::custom_value<Context> custom) : custom_(custom) {}
2455
2456 void format(parse_context<char_type>& parse_ctx, Context& ctx) const {
2457 custom_.format(custom_.value, parse_ctx, ctx);
2458 }
2459 };
2460
2461 constexpr basic_format_arg() : type_(detail::type::none_type) {}
2462 basic_format_arg(const detail::named_arg_info<char_type>* args, size_t size)
2463 : value_(args, size) {}
2464 template <typename T>
2465 basic_format_arg(T&& val)
2466 : value_(val), type_(detail::stored_type_constant<T, Context>::value) {}
2467
2468 constexpr explicit operator bool() const noexcept {
2469 return type_ != detail::type::none_type;
2470 }
2471 auto type() const -> detail::type { return type_; }
2472
2473 /**
2474 * Visits an argument dispatching to the appropriate visit method based on
2475 * the argument type. For example, if the argument type is `double` then
2476 * `vis(value)` will be called with the value of type `double`.
2477 */
2478 template <typename Visitor>
2479 FMT_CONSTEXPR FMT_INLINE auto visit(Visitor&& vis) const -> decltype(vis(0)) {
2480 using detail::map;
2481 switch (type_) {
2482 case detail::type::none_type: break;
2483 case detail::type::int_type: return vis(value_.int_value);
2484 case detail::type::uint_type: return vis(value_.uint_value);
2485 case detail::type::long_long_type: return vis(value_.long_long_value);
2486 case detail::type::ulong_long_type: return vis(value_.ulong_long_value);
2487 case detail::type::int128_type: return vis(map(value_.int128_value));
2488 case detail::type::uint128_type: return vis(map(value_.uint128_value));
2489 case detail::type::bool_type: return vis(value_.bool_value);
2490 case detail::type::char_type: return vis(value_.char_value);
2491 case detail::type::float_type: return vis(value_.float_value);
2492 case detail::type::double_type: return vis(value_.double_value);
2493 case detail::type::long_double_type: return vis(value_.long_double_value);
2494 case detail::type::cstring_type: return vis(value_.string.data);
2495 case detail::type::string_type: return vis(value_.string.str());
2496 case detail::type::pointer_type: return vis(value_.pointer);
2497 case detail::type::custom_type: return vis(handle(value_.custom));
2498 }
2499 return vis(monostate());
2500 }
2501
2502 auto format_custom(const char_type* parse_begin,
2503 parse_context<char_type>& parse_ctx, Context& ctx)
2504 -> bool {
2505 if (type_ != detail::type::custom_type) return false;
2506 parse_ctx.advance_to(parse_begin);
2507 value_.custom.format(value_.custom.value, parse_ctx, ctx);
2508 return true;
2509 }
2510};
2511
2512/**
2513 * A view of a collection of formatting arguments. To avoid lifetime issues it
2514 * should only be used as a parameter type in type-erased functions such as
2515 * `vformat`:
2516 *
2517 * void vlog(fmt::string_view fmt, fmt::format_args args); // OK
2518 * fmt::format_args args = fmt::make_format_args(); // Dangling reference
2519 */
2520template <typename Context> class basic_format_args {
2521 private:
2522 // A descriptor that contains information about formatting arguments.
2523 // If the number of arguments is less or equal to max_packed_args then
2524 // argument types are passed in the descriptor. This reduces binary code size
2525 // per formatting function call.
2526 ullong desc_;
2527 union {
2528 // If is_packed() returns true then argument values are stored in values_;
2529 // otherwise they are stored in args_. This is done to improve cache
2530 // locality and reduce compiled code size since storing larger objects
2531 // may require more code (at least on x86-64) even if the same amount of
2532 // data is actually copied to stack. It saves ~10% on the bloat test.
2533 const detail::value<Context>* values_;
2534 const basic_format_arg<Context>* args_;
2535 };
2536
2537 constexpr auto is_packed() const -> bool {
2538 return (desc_ & detail::is_unpacked_bit) == 0;
2539 }
2540 constexpr auto has_named_args() const -> bool {
2541 return (desc_ & detail::has_named_args_bit) != 0;
2542 }
2543
2544 FMT_CONSTEXPR auto type(int index) const -> detail::type {
2545 int shift = index * detail::packed_arg_bits;
2546 unsigned mask = (1 << detail::packed_arg_bits) - 1;
2547 return static_cast<detail::type>((desc_ >> shift) & mask);
2548 }
2549
2550 template <int NUM_ARGS, int NUM_NAMED_ARGS, ullong DESC>
2551 using store =
2552 detail::format_arg_store<Context, NUM_ARGS, NUM_NAMED_ARGS, DESC>;
2553
2554 public:
2555 using format_arg = basic_format_arg<Context>;
2556
2557 constexpr basic_format_args() : desc_(0), args_(nullptr) {}
2558
2559 /// Constructs a `basic_format_args` object from `format_arg_store`.
2560 template <int NUM_ARGS, int NUM_NAMED_ARGS, ullong DESC,
2561 FMT_ENABLE_IF(NUM_ARGS <= detail::max_packed_args)>
2562 constexpr FMT_ALWAYS_INLINE basic_format_args(
2563 const store<NUM_ARGS, NUM_NAMED_ARGS, DESC>& s)
2564 : desc_(DESC | (NUM_NAMED_ARGS != 0 ? +detail::has_named_args_bit : 0)),
2565 values_(s.args) {}
2566
2567 template <int NUM_ARGS, int NUM_NAMED_ARGS, ullong DESC,
2568 FMT_ENABLE_IF(NUM_ARGS > detail::max_packed_args)>
2569 constexpr basic_format_args(const store<NUM_ARGS, NUM_NAMED_ARGS, DESC>& s)
2570 : desc_(DESC | (NUM_NAMED_ARGS != 0 ? +detail::has_named_args_bit : 0)),
2571 args_(s.args) {}
2572
2573 /// Constructs a `basic_format_args` object from a dynamic list of arguments.
2574 constexpr basic_format_args(const format_arg* args, int count,
2575 bool has_named = false)
2576 : desc_(detail::is_unpacked_bit | detail::to_unsigned(count) |
2577 (has_named ? +detail::has_named_args_bit : 0)),
2578 args_(args) {}
2579
2580 /// Returns the argument with the specified id.
2581 FMT_CONSTEXPR auto get(int id) const -> format_arg {
2582 auto arg = format_arg();
2583 if (!is_packed()) {
2584 if (id < max_size()) arg = args_[id];
2585 return arg;
2586 }
2587 if (unsigned(id) >= detail::max_packed_args) return arg;
2588 arg.type_ = type(id);
2589 if (arg.type_ != detail::type::none_type) arg.value_ = values_[id];
2590 return arg;
2591 }
2592
2593 template <typename Char>
2594 auto get(basic_string_view<Char> name) const -> format_arg {
2595 int id = get_id(name);
2596 return id >= 0 ? get(id) : format_arg();
2597 }
2598
2599 template <typename Char>
2600 FMT_CONSTEXPR auto get_id(basic_string_view<Char> name) const -> int {
2601 if (!has_named_args()) return -1;
2602 const auto& named_args =
2603 (is_packed() ? values_[-1] : args_[-1].value_).named_args;
2604 for (size_t i = 0; i < named_args.size; ++i) {
2605 if (named_args.data[i].name == name) return named_args.data[i].id;
2606 }
2607 return -1;
2608 }
2609
2610 auto max_size() const -> int {
2611 return int(is_packed() ? ullong(detail::max_packed_args)
2612 : desc_ & ~detail::is_unpacked_bit);
2613 }
2614};
2615
2616// A formatting context.
2617class context {
2618 private:
2619 appender out_;
2620 format_args args_;
2621 FMT_NO_UNIQUE_ADDRESS locale_ref loc_;
2622
2623 public:
2624 using char_type = char; ///< The character type for the output.
2625 using iterator = appender;
2626 using format_arg = basic_format_arg<context>;
2627 enum { builtin_types = FMT_BUILTIN_TYPES };
2628
2629 /// Constructs a `context` object. References to the arguments are stored
2630 /// in the object so make sure they have appropriate lifetimes.
2631 constexpr context(iterator out, format_args args, locale_ref loc = {})
2632 : out_(out), args_(args), loc_(loc) {}
2633 context(context&&) = default;
2634 context(const context&) = delete;
2635 void operator=(const context&) = delete;
2636
2637 FMT_CONSTEXPR auto arg(int id) const -> format_arg { return args_.get(id); }
2638 inline auto arg(string_view name) const -> format_arg {
2639 return args_.get(name);
2640 }
2641 FMT_CONSTEXPR auto arg_id(string_view name) const -> int {
2642 return args_.get_id(name);
2643 }
2644 auto args() const -> const format_args& { return args_; }
2645
2646 // Returns an iterator to the beginning of the output range.
2647 constexpr auto out() const -> iterator { return out_; }
2648
2649 // Advances the begin iterator to `it`.
2650 FMT_CONSTEXPR void advance_to(iterator) {}
2651
2652 constexpr auto locale() const -> locale_ref { return loc_; }
2653};
2654
2655template <typename Char = char> struct runtime_format_string {
2656 basic_string_view<Char> str;
2657};
2658
2659/**
2660 * Creates a runtime format string.
2661 *
2662 * **Example**:
2663 *
2664 * // Check format string at runtime instead of compile-time.
2665 * fmt::print(fmt::runtime("{:d}"), "I am not a number");
2666 */
2667inline auto runtime(string_view s) -> runtime_format_string<> { return {{s}}; }
2668
2669/// A compile-time format string. Use `format_string` in the public API to
2670/// prevent type deduction.
2671template <typename... T> struct fstring {
2672 private:
2673 static constexpr int num_static_named_args =
2674 detail::count_static_named_args<T...>();
2675
2676 using checker = detail::format_string_checker<
2677 char, int(sizeof...(T)), num_static_named_args,
2678 num_static_named_args != detail::count_named_args<T...>()>;
2679
2680 using arg_pack = detail::arg_pack<T...>;
2681
2682 public:
2683 string_view str;
2684 using t = fstring;
2685
2686 // Reports a compile-time error if S is not a valid format string for T.
2687 template <size_t N>
2688 FMT_CONSTEVAL FMT_ALWAYS_INLINE fstring(const char (&s)[N]) : str(s, N - 1) {
2689 using namespace detail;
2690 static_assert(count<(is_view<remove_cvref_t<T>>::value &&
2691 std::is_reference<T>::value)...>() == 0,
2692 "passing views as lvalues is disallowed");
2693 if (FMT_USE_CONSTEVAL)
2694 parse_format_string<char>(str, checker(str, arg_pack()));
2695 constexpr bool unused = detail::enforce_compile_checks<sizeof(s) != 0>();
2696 (void)unused;
2697 }
2698 template <typename S,
2699 FMT_ENABLE_IF(std::is_convertible<const S&, string_view>::value)>
2700 FMT_CONSTEVAL FMT_ALWAYS_INLINE fstring(const S& s) : str(s) {
2701 if (FMT_USE_CONSTEVAL)
2702 detail::parse_format_string<char>(str, checker(str, arg_pack()));
2703 constexpr bool unused = detail::enforce_compile_checks<sizeof(s) != 0>();
2704 (void)unused;
2705 }
2706 template <typename S,
2707 FMT_ENABLE_IF(std::is_base_of<detail::compile_string, S>::value&&
2708 std::is_same<typename S::char_type, char>::value)>
2709 FMT_ALWAYS_INLINE fstring(const S&) : str(S()) {
2710 FMT_CONSTEXPR auto sv = string_view(S());
2711 FMT_CONSTEXPR int x = (parse_format_string(sv, checker(sv, arg_pack())), 0);
2712 detail::ignore_unused(x);
2713 }
2714 fstring(runtime_format_string<> fmt) : str(fmt.str) {}
2715
2716 FMT_DEPRECATED operator const string_view&() const { return str; }
2717 auto get() const -> string_view { return str; }
2718};
2719
2720template <typename... T> using format_string = typename fstring<T...>::t;
2721
2722template <typename T, typename Char = char>
2723using is_formattable = bool_constant<!std::is_same<
2724 detail::mapped_t<conditional_t<std::is_void<T>::value, int*, T>, Char>,
2725 void>::value>;
2726#if defined(__cpp_concepts) && __cpp_concepts >= 201907L
2727template <typename T, typename Char = char>
2728concept formattable = is_formattable<remove_reference_t<T>, Char>::value;
2729#endif
2730
2731// A formatter specialization for natively supported types.
2732template <typename T, typename Char>
2733struct formatter<T, Char,
2734 enable_if_t<detail::type_constant<T, Char>::value !=
2735 detail::type::custom_type>>
2736 : detail::native_formatter<T, Char, detail::type_constant<T, Char>::value> {
2737};
2738
2739/**
2740 * Constructs an object that stores references to arguments and can be
2741 * implicitly converted to `format_args`. `Context` can be omitted in which case
2742 * it defaults to `context`. See `arg` for lifetime considerations.
2743 */
2744// Take arguments by lvalue references to avoid some lifetime issues, e.g.
2745// auto args = make_format_args(std::string());
2746template <typename Context = context, typename... T,
2747 int NUM_ARGS = int(sizeof...(T)),
2748 int NUM_NAMED_ARGS = detail::count_named_args<T...>(),
2749 ullong DESC = detail::make_descriptor<Context, T...>()>
2750constexpr FMT_ALWAYS_INLINE auto make_format_args(T&... args)
2751 -> detail::format_arg_store<Context, NUM_ARGS, NUM_NAMED_ARGS, DESC> {
2752 return {{args...}};
2753}
2754
2755template <typename... T>
2756using vargs =
2757 detail::format_arg_store<context, int(sizeof...(T)),
2758 detail::count_named_args<T...>(),
2759 detail::make_descriptor<context, T...>()>;
2760
2761/**
2762 * Returns a named argument to be used in a formatting function.
2763 * It should only be used in a call to a formatting function.
2764 *
2765 * **Example**:
2766 *
2767 * fmt::print("The answer is {answer}.", fmt::arg("answer", 42));
2768 *
2769 * Named arguments passed with `fmt::arg` are not supported
2770 * in compile-time checks, but `"answer"_a=42` are compile-time checked in
2771 * sufficiently new compilers. See `operator""_a()`.
2772 */
2773template <typename T>
2774inline auto arg(const char* name, const T& arg) -> named_arg<T> {
2775 return {name, arg};
2776}
2777
2778/// Formats a string and writes the output to `out`.
2779template <typename OutputIt,
2780 FMT_ENABLE_IF(detail::is_output_iterator<remove_cvref_t<OutputIt>,
2781 char>::value)>
2782// DEPRECATED! Passing out as a forwarding reference.
2783auto vformat_to(OutputIt&& out, string_view fmt, format_args args)
2784 -> remove_cvref_t<OutputIt> {
2785 auto&& buf = detail::get_buffer<char>(out);
2786 detail::vformat_to(buf, fmt, args, {});
2787 return detail::get_iterator(buf, out);
2788}
2789
2790/**
2791 * Formats `args` according to specifications in `fmt`, writes the result to
2792 * the output iterator `out` and returns the iterator past the end of the output
2793 * range. `format_to` does not append a terminating null character.
2794 *
2795 * **Example**:
2796 *
2797 * auto out = std::vector<char>();
2798 * fmt::format_to(std::back_inserter(out), "{}", 42);
2799 */
2800template <typename OutputIt, typename... T,
2801 FMT_ENABLE_IF(detail::is_output_iterator<remove_cvref_t<OutputIt>,
2802 char>::value)>
2803FMT_INLINE auto format_to(OutputIt&& out, format_string<T...> fmt, T&&... args)
2804 -> remove_cvref_t<OutputIt> {
2805 return vformat_to(out, fmt.str, vargs<T...>{{args...}});
2806}
2807
2808template <typename OutputIt> struct format_to_n_result {
2809 OutputIt out; ///< Iterator past the end of the output range.
2810 size_t size; ///< Total (not truncated) output size.
2811};
2812
2813template <typename OutputIt, typename... T,
2814 FMT_ENABLE_IF(detail::is_output_iterator<OutputIt, char>::value)>
2815auto vformat_to_n(OutputIt out, size_t n, string_view fmt, format_args args)
2816 -> format_to_n_result<OutputIt> {
2817 using traits = detail::fixed_buffer_traits;
2818 auto buf = detail::iterator_buffer<OutputIt, char, traits>(out, n);
2819 detail::vformat_to(buf, fmt, args, {});
2820 return {buf.out(), buf.count()};
2821}
2822
2823/**
2824 * Formats `args` according to specifications in `fmt`, writes up to `n`
2825 * characters of the result to the output iterator `out` and returns the total
2826 * (not truncated) output size and the iterator past the end of the output
2827 * range. `format_to_n` does not append a terminating null character.
2828 */
2829template <typename OutputIt, typename... T,
2830 FMT_ENABLE_IF(detail::is_output_iterator<OutputIt, char>::value)>
2831FMT_INLINE auto format_to_n(OutputIt out, size_t n, format_string<T...> fmt,
2832 T&&... args) -> format_to_n_result<OutputIt> {
2833 return vformat_to_n(out, n, fmt.str, vargs<T...>{{args...}});
2834}
2835
2836struct format_to_result {
2837 char* out; ///< Pointer to just after the last successful write.
2838 bool truncated; ///< Specifies if the output was truncated.
2839
2840 FMT_CONSTEXPR operator char*() const {
2841 // Report truncation to prevent silent data loss.
2842 if (truncated) report_error("output is truncated");
2843 return out;
2844 }
2845};
2846
2847template <size_t N>
2848FMT_DEPRECATED auto vformat_to(char (&out)[N], string_view fmt,
2849 format_args args) -> format_to_result {
2850 auto result = vformat_to_n(out, N, fmt, args);
2851 return {result.out, result.size > N};
2852}
2853
2854template <size_t N, typename... T>
2855FMT_INLINE auto format_to(char (&out)[N], format_string<T...> fmt, T&&... args)
2856 -> format_to_result {
2857 auto result = vformat_to_n(out, N, fmt.str, vargs<T...>{{args...}});
2858 return {result.out, result.size > N};
2859}
2860
2861/// Returns the number of chars in the output of `format(fmt, args...)`.
2862template <typename... T>
2863FMT_NODISCARD FMT_INLINE auto formatted_size(format_string<T...> fmt,
2864 T&&... args) -> size_t {
2865 auto buf = detail::counting_buffer<>();
2866 detail::vformat_to(buf, fmt.str, vargs<T...>{{args...}}, {});
2867 return buf.count();
2868}
2869
2870FMT_API void vprint(string_view fmt, format_args args);
2871FMT_API void vprint(FILE* f, string_view fmt, format_args args);
2872FMT_API void vprintln(FILE* f, string_view fmt, format_args args);
2873FMT_API void vprint_buffered(FILE* f, string_view fmt, format_args args);
2874
2875/**
2876 * Formats `args` according to specifications in `fmt` and writes the output
2877 * to `stdout`.
2878 *
2879 * **Example**:
2880 *
2881 * fmt::print("The answer is {}.", 42);
2882 */
2883template <typename... T>
2884FMT_INLINE void print(format_string<T...> fmt, T&&... args) {
2885 vargs<T...> va = {{args...}};
2886 if FMT_CONSTEXPR20 (!detail::use_utf8)
2887 return detail::vprint_mojibake(stdout, fmt.str, va, false);
2888 detail::is_locking<T...>() ? vprint_buffered(stdout, fmt.str, va)
2889 : vprint(fmt.str, va);
2890}
2891
2892/**
2893 * Formats `args` according to specifications in `fmt` and writes the
2894 * output to the file `f`.
2895 *
2896 * **Example**:
2897 *
2898 * fmt::print(stderr, "Don't {}!", "panic");
2899 */
2900template <typename... T>
2901FMT_INLINE void print(FILE* f, format_string<T...> fmt, T&&... args) {
2902 vargs<T...> va = {{args...}};
2903 if FMT_CONSTEXPR20 (!detail::use_utf8)
2904 return detail::vprint_mojibake(f, fmt.str, va, false);
2905 detail::is_locking<T...>() ? vprint_buffered(f, fmt.str, va)
2906 : vprint(f, fmt.str, va);
2907}
2908
2909/// Formats `args` according to specifications in `fmt` and writes the output
2910/// to the file `f` followed by a newline.
2911template <typename... T>
2912FMT_INLINE void println(FILE* f, format_string<T...> fmt, T&&... args) {
2913 vargs<T...> va = {{args...}};
2914 if FMT_CONSTEXPR20 (detail::use_utf8) return vprintln(f, fmt.str, va);
2915 detail::vprint_mojibake(f, fmt.str, va, true);
2916}
2917
2918/// Formats `args` according to specifications in `fmt` and writes the output
2919/// to `stdout` followed by a newline.
2920template <typename... T>
2921FMT_INLINE void println(format_string<T...> fmt, T&&... args) {
2922 fmt::println(stdout, fmt, static_cast<T&&>(args)...);
2923}
2924
2925FMT_PRAGMA_GCC(pop_options)
2926FMT_END_EXPORT
2927FMT_END_NAMESPACE
2928
